10.1016/j.jmp.2015.11.006
Fechnerian scaling is a method to compute distances between physical stimuli (such as the amplitude–frequency space of tone) from the probabilities with which each of these stimuli can be discriminated from its very close neighbors.  Dzhafarov and Colonius (1999)  proposed a theory of Fechnerian scaling based on a multidimensional stimulus space  
                         M 
                       and on a metric function  
                         F 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               x 
                            
                            , 
                            
                            
                               u 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                      , named  Fechner–Finsler metric function , where  
                         
                            x 
                         
                       is a point of  
                         M 
                       and  
                         
                            u 
                         
                       is a vector in the tangent space  
                         
                            
                               T 
                            
                            
                               
                                  x 
                               
                            
                         
                         M 
                         ∖ 
                         
                            { 
                            
                               0 
                            
                            } 
                         
                       at

                         ∈ 
                         M

                         F

Dzhafarov and Colonius (1999, 2001)  developed their theory of multidimensional Fechnerian scaling from three assumptions (the latest version of the generalized Fechnerian scaling can be found in  Dzhafarov, 2008a,b  and  Dzhafarov & Colonius, 2007 ). We prove (Section   2 ) that these three assumptions imply that the function  
                         F 
                       is a  metric function , i.e.,  
                         F 
                       satisfies the following three conditions:

(B)  
                         F 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               x 
                            
                            , 
                            
                            
                               u 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       is continuous as function of the  
                         2 
                         n 
                       variables ( 
                         
                            x 
                         
                         , 
                         
                         
                            u 
                         
                      ).

(C)  
                         F 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               x 
                            
                            , 
                            
                            
                               u 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       is positive definite, i.e.,  
                         F 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               x 
                            
                            , 
                            
                            
                               u 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                         > 
                         0 
                       for all  
                         
                            u 
                         
                         ≠ 
                         
                            0 
                         
                      .

(D)  
                         F 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               x 
                            
                            , 
                            
                            
                               u 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       is positively homogeneous (of order one in  
                         
                            u 
                         
                      ), i.e.,  
                         F 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               x 
                            
                            , 
                            k 
                            
                               u 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                         = 
                         k 
                         F 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               x 
                            
                            , 
                            
                            
                               u 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       for  
                         k 
                         > 
                         0 
                      .

In this paper, we consider that the stimulus space is a differentiable manifold  
                         M 
                       in  
                         
                            
                               R 
                            
                            
                               n 
                            
                         
                      , i.e., for each point  
                         
                            x 
                         
                         ∈ 
                         M 
                       there is some neighborhood  
                         U 
                       of  
                         
                            x 
                         
                       and some integer  
                         q 
                      , with  
                         0 
                         ≤ 
                         q 
                         ≤ 
                         n 
                      , such that  
                         U 
                       is diffeomorphic to a subset of  
                         
                            
                               R 
                            
                            
                               q

                         M 
                       at  
                         
                            x 
                         
                       is  
                         
                            
                               R 
                            
                            
                               q

                               R

                         M 
                       at all points  
                         
                            y 
                         
                         ∈ 
                         U 
                       ( Spivak, 1999 , p. 64). The latter allows us to define polygonal arcs from  
                         
                            x 
                         
                       to  
                         
                            x 
                         
                         + 
                         
                            v 
                         
                         s

                         0

                         U 
                      . These polygonal arcs, that we call  polygonal arcs  ( in the small )  for

Equivalently,  Dzhafarov and Colonius (2001)  defined Fechnerian geodesic arc in the small as an arc connecting  
                         
                            x 
                         
                       to  
                         
                            x 
                         
                         + 
                         
                            v 
                         
                         s 
                      , whose psychometric length tends to the Fechnerian distance  
                         G 
                         
                            (

                               0 
                            
                            
                               + 
                            
                         
                      . These authors remark that it is important to investigate the existence and properties of the Fechnerian geodesic arcs in the small. In this sense, the principal aim of our work is to characterize the Fechnerian geodesic arcs in the small. As we will show, this characterization establishes useful relationships between Fechnerian geodesic arcs in the small and the shapes of the indicatrices.

We say that a set

                         F 
                       with respect to the direction of any tangent vector

                      , but not necessarily vice versa. In Section   8 , we illustrate with an example the characterization of geodesic arcs in the small on a psychological space. Section   9  contains a brief discussion where we conclude that the characterization of Fechnerian geodesic arcs in the small can be used to find the shapes of the Fechnerian indicatrices.

We use the following notation. A stimulus space is represented by a pathwise connected differentiable manifold

                       in

                               n

                         ≔

                               M

                       be the tangent bundle of

                      . Each element of  TM  has the form (

                         ,

                      , where  
                         a 
                         < 
                         b 
                       are real numbers,  
                         
                            x 
                         
                         
                            ( 
                            a 
                            ) 
                         
                         = 
                         
                            a 
                         
                       and  
                         
                            x 
                         
                         
                            ( 
                            b 
                            ) 
                         
                         = 
                         
                            b 
                         
                      . The directed image  
                         C 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               a 
                            
                            , 
                            
                            
                               b 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                         ⊆

                       of the path  
                         
                            x 
                         
                         : 
                         
                            [ 
                            a 
                            , 
                            
                            b 
                            ] 
                         
                         → 
                         M 
                       is called an  arc  or  curve  connecting  
                         
                            a 
                         
                       to  
                         
                            b 
                         
                      . Throughout this paper, an arc will mean a directed arc. An arc  
                         C 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               a 
                            
                            , 
                            
                            
                               b 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                         ⊆ 
                         M

                               C 
                            
                            
                               1

                            x 
                         
                         : 
                         
                            [ 
                            a 
                            , 
                            
                            b 
                            ] 
                         
                         → 
                         M 
                       with a bounded derivative  
                         
                            
                               
                                  x 
                               
                            
                            
                               ̇

                            [ 
                            a 
                            , 
                            
                            b 
                            ] 
                         
                      . The set of all the piecewise  
                         
                            
                               C 
                            
                            
                               1 
                            
                         
                       arcs on  
                         M 
                       is denoted by  
                         Ω 
                      . For the sake of simplicity, the set of all the piecewise  
                         
                            
                               C 
                            
                            
                               1 
                            
                         
                       arcs connecting  
                         
                            a 
                         
                       to  
                         
                            b 
                         
                       and the set of parametric representations of these arcs are denoted by  
                         
                            
                               Ω 
                            
                            
                               
                                  [ 
                                  
                                     a 
                                  
                                  , 
                                  
                                  
                                     b 
                                  
                                  ] 
                               
                            
                         
                      . The pathwise connectedness of  
                         M 
                       means that any two points of this stimulus space can be connected by a  
                         
                            
                               C 
                            
                            
                               1 
                            
                         
                       arc.

The theory of Fechnerian scaling proposed by  Dzhafarov and Colonius (1999, 2001)  considers a multidimensional stimulus space  
                         M 
                       ( Kozma & Tamàssy, 2003 ), and it is based on three assumptions:

(i) The first assumption is the existence of a continuous non-negative function  
                         ψ 
                         : 
                         M 
                         × 
                         M 
                         → 
                         R 
                      , called  psychometric function , where  
                         ψ 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               x 
                            
                            , 
                            
                            
                               y 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                      , denoted by  
                         
                            
                               ψ 
                            
                            
                               
                                  x 
                               
                            
                         
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               y 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       to stress that  
                         
                            x 
                         
                       is a fixed reference stimulus, means the probability with which  
                         
                            y 
                         
                       is discriminated from  
                         
                            x 
                         
                      , and satisfies that  
                         
                            
                               ψ 
                            
                            
                               
                                  x 
                               
                            
                         
                         
                            ( 
                            ⋅ 
                            ) 
                         
                       has an unique global minimum.

(ii) The second assumption is the existence of a continuous transformation  
                         Φ 
                         : 
                         
                            [ 
                            0 
                            , 
                            ε 
                            ) 
                         
                         → 
                         
                            [ 
                            0 
                            , 
                            ∞ 
                            ) 
                         
                       with  
                         Φ 
                         
                            ( 
                            0 
                            ) 
                         
                         = 
                         0 
                       such that the following limit exists for any  
                         
                            x 
                         
                         ∈ 
                         M 
                       and  
                         
                            v 
                         
                         ∈ 
                         
                            
                               T 
                            
                            
                               
                                  x

                         ∖ 
                         
                            {

                            }

                               F

                                     u 
                                  
                                  )

                                           0

(iii) The third assumption is the symmetry of the Fechnerian distance, i.e., the Fechnerian distance from  
                         
                            a 
                         
                       to  
                         
                            b

In our work, assumption (iii) is not required (in fact, this was abandoned in  Dzhafarov, 2002 ). In what follows, we suppose that the function  
                         F 
                         : 
                         T 
                         M 
                         → 
                         R 
                       satisfies the above mentioned conditions (A), (B), (C) and (D).

The convexity of  
                         F 
                       plays a central role in the Fechner–Finsler geometry. We say that a function  
                         F 
                         : 
                         T 
                         M 
                         → 
                         R

                            x 
                         
                         ∈ 
                         M 
                       if  
                         F 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               x 
                            
                            , 
                            α 
                            
                               v 
                            
                            + 
                            
                               ( 
                               1 
                               − 
                               α 
                               ) 
                            
                            
                               w 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                         ≤ 
                         α 
                         F 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               x 
                            
                            , 
                            
                            
                               v 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                         + 
                         
                            ( 
                            1 
                            − 
                            α 
                            )

                            (

                               w

                         ∖

                            }

                         ∈

                            ]

This definition takes a more simple form if we consider that function

                               v 
                            
                            )

                               u 
                            
                            )

                       is convex at  
                         
                            x 
                         
                       if, and only if,  
                         F 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               x 
                            
                            , 
                            
                            
                               v 
                            
                            + 
                            
                               w 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                         ≤ 
                         F 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               x 
                            
                            , 
                            
                            
                               v 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                         + 
                         F 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               x 
                            
                            , 
                            
                            
                               w 
                            
                            )

                            v 
                         
                         , 
                         
                         
                            w 
                         
                         ∈ 
                         
                            
                               T 
                            
                            
                               
                                  x 
                               
                            
                         
                         M 
                         ∖ 
                         
                            { 
                            
                               0 
                            
                            } 
                         
                      . Then, property (D) allows us to define the convexity of  
                         F 
                       in the direction of a vector  
                         
                            v 
                         
                         ∈ 
                         
                            
                               T 
                            
                            
                               
                                  x

                         ∖ 
                         
                            { 
                            
                               0 
                            
                            } 
                         
                      . We introduce the following definition of convexity of  
                         F 
                       in a given direction  
                         
                            v 
                         
                      .  
                         Definition 2 
                         Let  
                               F 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  
                                     x 
                                  
                                  , 
                                  
                                  
                                     u 
                                  
                                  ) 
                               
                             be a positively homogeneous function of order one in  
                               
                                  u 
                               
                            . We say that  
                               F 
                             is  convex at  
                            
                               
                                  x 
                               
                               ∈ 
                               M 
                             
                            in the direction of  
                            
                               
                                  v 
                               
                               ∈ 
                               
                                  
                                     T 
                                  
                                  
                                     
                                        x 
                                     
                                  
                               
                               M 
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     0 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                             (or  the direction of  
                            
                               
                                  v 
                               
                             
                            is a convex direction of  
                            
                               F 
                            ) if  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                     = 
                                     
                                        
                                           ∑ 
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    u 
                                                 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                           ∈ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 H 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              u 
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ′ 
                                        
                                     
                                     ⇒ 
                                     F 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                        , 
                                        
                                           v 
                                        
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     ≤ 
                                     
                                        
                                           ∑ 
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    u 
                                                 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                           ∈ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 H 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     F 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                        , 
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 u 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              ′ 
                                           
                                        
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     
                                     ∀ 
                                     
                                        
                                           H 
                                        
                                        
                                           ′ 
                                        
                                     
                                     ⊆ 
                                     
                                        
                                           T 
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              x 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     M 
                                     ∖ 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        
                                           0 
                                        
                                        } 
                                     
                                     .

Let  
                               F 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  
                                     x 
                                  
                                  , 
                                  
                                  
                                     u 
                                  
                                  ) 
                               
                             be a positively homogeneous function of order one in  
                               
                                  u 
                               
                            . We say that  
                               F 
                             is  convex at  
                            
                               
                                  x 
                               
                               ∈ 
                               M 
                             
                            in the direction of  
                            
                               
                                  v 
                               
                               ∈ 
                               
                                  
                                     T 
                                  
                                  
                                     
                                        x 
                                     
                                  
                               
                               M 
                               ∖ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     0 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                             (or  the direction of  
                            
                               
                                  v 
                               
                             
                            is a convex direction of  
                            
                               F 
                            ) if  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                     = 
                                     
                                        
                                           ∑ 
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    u 
                                                 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                           ∈ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 H 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              u 
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ′ 
                                        
                                     
                                     ⇒ 
                                     F 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                        , 
                                        
                                           v 
                                        
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     ≤ 
                                     
                                        
                                           ∑ 
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    u 
                                                 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                           ∈ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 H 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     F 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                        , 
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 u 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              ′ 
                                           
                                        
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     
                                     ∀ 
                                     
                                        
                                           H 
                                        
                                        
                                           ′ 
                                        
                                     
                                     ⊆ 
                                     
                                        
                                           T 
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              x 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     M 
                                     ∖ 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        
                                           0 
                                        
                                        } 
                                     
                                     .

Thus, to say that the direction of  
                         
                            v 
                         
                       at  
                         
                            x 
                         
                       is a convex direction of  
                         F 
                       amounts to saying that the straight line segment from  
                         
                            x 
                         
                       to  
                         
                            x 
                         
                         + 
                         
                            v 
                         
                         s 
                       is a Fechnerian geodesic arc from  
                         
                            x 
                         
                       to  
                         
                            x 
                         
                         + 
                         
                            v 
                         
                         s 
                      , for  
                         s 
                         > 
                         0 
                       sufficiently small.

If  
                         F 
                       is convex in the direction of  
                         
                            v 
                         
                      , then by (D), it is convex in the direction of  
                         α 
                         
                            v 
                         
                      ,  
                         α 
                         > 
                         0 
                      . The convexity of  
                         F 
                       at  
                         
                            x 
                         
                       in the direction of  
                         
                            v 
                         
                       is said to be  strict  if  
                         
                            u 
                         
                         + 
                         
                            w 
                         
                         = 
                         
                            v 
                         
                         ⇒ 
                         F 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               x 
                            
                            , 
                            
                            
                               v 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                         < 
                         F 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               x 
                            
                            , 
                            
                            
                               u 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                         + 
                         F 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               x 
                            
                            , 
                            
                            
                               w 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                      , for all  
                         
                            u 
                         
                         , 
                         
                         
                            w 
                         
                         ∈ 
                         
                            
                               T 
                            
                            
                               
                                  x 
                               
                            
                         
                         M 
                         ∖ 
                         
                            { 
                            
                               0 
                            
                            } 
                         
                       such that  
                         
                            u 
                         
                         ≠ 
                         α 
                         
                            w 
                         
                      ,  
                         α 
                         > 
                         0 
                      ,  
                         
                            u 
                         
                         ≠ 
                         
                            0 
                         
                       and  
                         
                            w 
                         
                         ≠ 
                         
                            0 
                         
                      . When the convexity of  
                         F 
                       in the direction of  
                         
                            v 
                         
                       is not strict, we say that the convexity is  weak .  
                         F 
                       ( strictly )  convex  means that  
                         F 
                       is (strictly) convex at all  
                         
                            x 
                         
                         ∈ 
                         M 
                       for all  
                         
                            v 
                         
                         ∈ 
                         
                            
                               T 
                            
                            
                               
                                  x 
                               
                            
                         
                         M 
                         ∖ 
                         
                            { 
                            
                               0 
                            
                            } 
                         
                      . In the Finsler geometry, generally it is supposed that  
                         F 
                       is regular (strictly convex) for all directions at all  
                         
                            x 
                         
                         ∈ 
                         M 
                       in order to guarantee the differentiability of the solutions of  (3)  (see, e.g.,  Busemann & Mayer, 1941 ).

As far as we know,  Definition 2  of convexity of a metric function  
                         F 
                       in a given direction has not been addressed in the literature.  Busemann and Mayer (1941, p. 181) , and  Dzhafarov and Colonius (2001, p. 700)  define convexity (quasi-regularity) of  
                         F 
                         
                            ( 
                            
                               x 
                            
                            , 
                            
                            
                               v 
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       at  
                         
                            x 
                         
                       in the direction  
                         
                            v 
                         
                       in terms of the indicatrix of  
                         F 
                      , but not in terms of  
                         F 
                      .

Unlike the Finsler geometry, in the multidimensional Fechnerian metric theory of Dzhafarov–Colonius, the convexity of  
                         F 
                       is not required. In this case, if the direction of  
                         
                            v 
                         
                       at  
                         
                            x 
                         
                       is not a convex direction of  
                         F 
                      , then for  
                         s 
                         > 
                         0 
                       sufficiently small, the Fechnerian geodesic arcs from  
                         
                            x 
                         
                       to  
                         
                            x 
                         
                         + 
                         
                            v 
                         
                         s 
                       have a psychometric length strictly less than the length of the straight line segment from

Using the results obtained in this section, we can prove the following theorem:  
                         Theorem 1 
                         Properties of the F-minimizing Sets of Tangent Vectors 
                         
                            
                               
                                  (a) 
                                  
                                     Each subset of an  
                                     
                                        F 
                                     
                                     -minimizing set of tangent vectors  
                                     
                                        H 
                                        ⊆ 
                                        
                                           
                                              T 
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 x

                                        ∖ 
                                        
                                           { 
                                           
                                              0 
                                           
                                           } 
                                        
                                      
                                     is also an  
                                     
                                        F 
                                     
                                     -minimizing set of tangent vectors at  
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                     
                                     , and in particular, if  
                                     
                                        
                                           u 
                                        
                                        ∈ 
                                        H 
                                     
                                     , then  
                                     
                                        
                                           { 
                                           
                                              u 
                                           
                                           } 
                                        
                                      
                                     is an  
                                     
                                        F 
                                     
                                     -minimizing set of tangent vectors at  
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                      
                                     ; 
                                  
                               
                               
                                  (b) 
                                  
                                     The set of all the  
                                     
                                        F 
                                     
                                     -minimizing sets of tangent vectors at  
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                      
                                     with sum  
                                     
                                        
                                           v 
                                        
                                      
                                     is closed under convex combinations; 
                                  
                               
                               
                                  (c) 
                                  
                                     Every  
                                     
                                        F 
                                     
                                     -minimizing set of tangent vectors with sum  
                                     
                                        
                                           v 
                                        
                                      
                                     is a convex combination of extreme  
                                     
                                        F 
                                     
                                     -minimizing set of tangent vectors with sum  
                                     
                                        
                                           v 
                                        
                                     
                                     . 
                                  
                               
                            
                         
                         
                            By part   (c)   of     
                               Theorem   1 
                            , the characterization of Fechnerian geodesic arcs in the small for  
                            
                               
                                  v 
                               
                             
                            at  
                            
                               
                                  x 
                               
                             
                            is reduced to the characterization of extreme  
                            
                               F 
                            
                            -minimizing set of tangent vectors at  
                            
                               
                                  x 
                               
                             
                            with sum  
                            
                               
                                  v 
                               
                            
                            .

(a) 
                                  
                                     Each subset of an  
                                     
                                        F 
                                     
                                     -minimizing set of tangent vectors  
                                     
                                        H 
                                        ⊆

                                           u 
                                        
                                        ∈ 
                                        H 
                                     
                                     , then  
                                     
                                        
                                           { 
                                           
                                              u 
                                           
                                           } 
                                        
                                      
                                     is an  
                                     
                                        F 
                                     
                                     -minimizing set of tangent vectors at  
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                      
                                     ;

                                     The set of all the  
                                     
                                        F 
                                     
                                     -minimizing sets of tangent vectors at  
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                        
                                      
                                     with sum  
                                     
                                        
                                           v 
                                        
                                      
                                     is closed under convex combinations;

                                        F

By part   (c)   of     
                               Theorem   1

A subset  
                         S 
                       of  
                         
                            
                               R 
                            
                            
                               n 
                            
                         
                       is said to be  convex  if  
                         λ 
                         
                            u 
                         
                         + 
                         
                            ( 
                            1 
                            − 
                            λ 
                            ) 
                         
                         
                            v 
                         
                         ∈ 
                         S 
                       whenever  
                         
                            u 
                         
                      ,  
                         
                            v 
                         
                         ∈ 
                         S 
                       and  
                         0 
                         < 
                         λ 
                         < 
                         1 
                      . The intersection of all the convex sets containing a given subset  
                         S 
                       of  
                         
                            
                               R 
                            
                            
                               n 
                            
                         
                       is called the  convex closure  of  
                         S 
                       and is denoted by conv  
                         S 
                      . The convex closure of  
                         S 
                       is a convex set because the intersection of an arbitrary collection of convex sets is convex ( Rockafellar, 1970 , p. 10). We use the fact that for any set  
                         S 
                       of  
                         
                            
                               R 
                            
                            
                               n 
                            
                         
                      , its corresponding conv  
                         S 
                       consists of all the convex combinations of the elements of  
                         S 
                       ( Rockafellar, 1970 , p. 12), i.e., conv  
                         S 
                         = 
                         
                            {

                                  m

                                     x

                            S

                            0

                            = 
                            1

                            ⋯

                            1 
                            , 
                              with

                            N 
                            }
